task_completion.description: LibreOffice Impress is open with a presentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Impress application is visible on the screen, displaying a presentation with slides.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the PDF export functionality.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'File' menu is open with 'Export As' -> 'Export as PDF...' selected, or the PDF export icon is clicked.

task_completion.description: The PDF Options dialog is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'PDF Options' is visible, allowing the user to configure PDF export settings.

task_completion.description: The file save/export dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file browser dialog (e.g., 'Export') is open to choose the save location and filename, with the file type set to PDF.

task_completion.description: The file is saved to the specified location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'presentation.pdf' (or 'export_test.pdf') is entered in the save dialog in the Documents (or Presentations) folder, and the Save/Export button is clicked.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice GUI to export the PDF.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands like 'soffice --headless --convert-to pdf'.

integrity.description: The agent actually exported the file rather than just renaming an existing file or creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PDF Options and Export dialogs are actively used in LibreOffice.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The presentation is synthetically created with 3 empty slides named Slide1, Slide2, and Slide3. The task description asks to save the PDF to '/home/ga/Documents/presentation.pdf', but the underlying system verifier expects it at '/home/ga/Documents/Presentations/export_test.pdf'. Please accept either file path as correct. The task description mentions 'specified quality settings', but no specific settings are required; simply exporting with default PDF options is sufficient.
